item.title: Test Project — AI Playground
item.description:
This is a test project to verify the project pages render correctly.

Features

  • Interactive AI experiments
  • Multi-model comparison
  • Real-time inference demos

item.title: Testing Multi-Language Content — Hello World
item.description:
This is a test blog post to verify the site builds correctly with English content.

What We’re Testing

  • Hugo build pipeline
  • Tailwind CSS compilation
  • Pagefind search indexing
  • Multi-language content (EN/ZH)
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD deployment

Expected Results

All pages should render correctly with:

  • Proper CSS styling
  • Dark/light theme toggle
  • Navigation menu
  • Search functionality

item.title: Testing Multi-Language Content — Hello World
item.description:
This is a test blog post to verify the site builds correctly with English content.

What We’re Testing

  • Hugo build pipeline
  • Tailwind CSS compilation
  • Pagefind search indexing
  • Multi-language content (EN/ZH)
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD deployment

Expected Results

All pages should render correctly with:

  • Proper CSS styling
  • Dark/light theme toggle
  • Navigation menu
  • Search functionality

item.title: 测试多语言内容 — 你好世界
item.description:
这是一篇测试博客文章，用来验证站点是否正确构建了中文内容。

我们测试什么

  • Hugo 构建流水线
  • Tailwind CSS 编译
  • Pagefind 搜索索引
  • 多语言内容（EN/ZH）
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD 部署

预期结果

所有页面应该正确渲染，包括：

  • 正确的 CSS 样式
  • 暗色/亮色主题切换
  • 导航菜单
  • 搜索功能

item.title: 测试论文 — 多模态学习综述
item.description:
这是一篇测试论文，用来验证出版物页面的正确渲染。

摘要

本文综述了多模态学习的最新进展，包括视觉-语言模型、跨模态检索和多模态推理等技术。

贡献

  • 系统地整理了多模态学习方法
  • 提供了全面的实验对比
  • 开放了测试代码和数据集

item.title: The Future of Multimodal AI: From Transformers to Reasoning Agents
item.description:
The field of artificial intelligence is undergoing a fundamental shift. We’re moving beyond simple pattern recognition toward systems that can reason, plan, and interact with the world in increasingly sophisticated ways.

The Rise of Multimodal Reasoning

Recent advances in vision-language models have demonstrated that combining multiple modalities isn’t just about having more data — it’s about enabling richer forms of reasoning. Models that can simultaneously process text, images, audio, and sensor data develop a more grounded understanding of the world.

From Chatbots to Agents

The next frontier is autonomous AI agents — systems that can perceive their environment, make decisions, take actions, and learn from the consequences. This represents a paradigm shift from reactive chatbot interfaces to proactive, goal-directed AI systems.

Key Challenges

  1. Reliability: Agents must make correct decisions consistently, not just probabilistically
  2. Safety: Autonomous systems need robust safeguards and oversight mechanisms
  3. Efficiency: Running complex reasoning at scale requires breakthroughs in both algorithms and hardware

The journey ahead is exciting, and the research community is uniquely positioned to shape how these powerful systems evolve.

item.title: The Future of Multimodal AI: From Transformers to Reasoning Agents
item.description:
The field of artificial intelligence is undergoing a fundamental shift. We’re moving beyond simple pattern recognition toward systems that can reason, plan, and interact with the world in increasingly sophisticated ways.

The Rise of Multimodal Reasoning

Recent advances in vision-language models have demonstrated that combining multiple modalities isn’t just about having more data — it’s about enabling richer forms of reasoning. Models that can simultaneously process text, images, audio, and sensor data develop a more grounded understanding of the world.

From Chatbots to Agents

The next frontier is autonomous AI agents — systems that can perceive their environment, make decisions, take actions, and learn from the consequences. This represents a paradigm shift from reactive chatbot interfaces to proactive, goal-directed AI systems.

Key Challenges

  1. Reliability: Agents must make correct decisions consistently, not just probabilistically
  2. Safety: Autonomous systems need robust safeguards and oversight mechanisms
  3. Efficiency: Running complex reasoning at scale requires breakthroughs in both algorithms and hardware

The journey ahead is exciting, and the research community is uniquely positioned to shape how these powerful systems evolve.

item.title: 多模态AI的未来：从Transformer到推理智能体
item.description:
人工智能领域正在经历一场根本性的变革。我们正从简单的模式识别迈向能够推理、规划和与世界以越来越复杂方式交互的系统。

多模态推理的兴起

最近视觉语言模型的进展表明，结合多种模态不仅仅是拥有更多数据——而是实现更丰富的推理形式。能够同时处理文本、图像、音频和传感器数据的模型发展出更加扎实的世界理解能力。

从聊天机器人到智能体

下一个前沿是自主AI智能体——能够感知环境、做出决策、采取行动并从结果中学习。这代表了从被动聊天机器人接口向主动、目标导向AI系统的范式转变。

item.title: Attention Is All You Need: A Survey of Transformer Architectures
2025年参数高效微调实战指南
item.description:
参数高效微调（PEFT）已成为适配大型语言模型到特定任务的必备技术，无需全量微调的计算成本。

为什么PEFT很重要

全量微调一个700亿参数的模型需要数百个GPU和数天训练。LoRA等PEFT方法用1000倍更少的可训练参数实现了相当的性能。

方法对比

LoRA（低秩自适应）

在transformer层中注入可训练的低秩分解矩阵，同时保持预训练权重冻结。简单、有效、广泛采用。

QLoRA（量化LoRA）

将4位量化与LoRA结合，可以在单个GPU上微调650亿参数模型。

AdaLoRA

根据重要性评分自适应分配参数预算，用更少参数实现更好的性能。

item.title: Practical Guide to Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning in 2025
item.description:
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) has become essential for adapting large language models to specific tasks without the computational cost of full fine-tuning.

Why PEFT Matters

Full fine-tuning of a 70B parameter model requires hundreds of GPUs and days of training. PEFT methods like LoRA achieve comparable performance with 1000x fewer trainable parameters.

Methods Compared

LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)

Injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into transformer layers while keeping pre-trained weights frozen. Simple, effective, widely adopted.

QLoRA (Quantized LoRA)

Combines 4-bit quantization with LoRA, enabling fine-tuning of 65B parameter models on a single GPU.

AdaLoRA

Adaptively allocates parameter budgets based on importance scoring, achieving better performance with fewer parameters.

IA³ (Infused Adapter by Inhibiting and Amplifying Inner Activations)

Scales inner activations of pretrained models with three tiny learnable vectors per layer.

Code Example

from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model

config = LoraConfig(
 r=16,
 lora_alpha=32,
 target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],
 lora_dropout=0.1,
)
model = get_peft_model(base_model, config)
model.print_trainable_parameters()
# trainable params: 4,194,304 || all params: 7,424,243,712

Benchmark Results

On the GLUE benchmark, LoRA achieves 98.2% of full fine-tuning performance with 0.1% of trainable parameters. QLoRA maintains this with 4-bit quantization, reducing memory usage by 75%.

item.title: Practical Guide to Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning in 2025
item.description:
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) has become essential for adapting large language models to specific tasks without the computational cost of full fine-tuning.

Why PEFT Matters

Full fine-tuning of a 70B parameter model requires hundreds of GPUs and days of training. PEFT methods like LoRA achieve comparable performance with 1000x fewer trainable parameters.

Methods Compared

LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)

Injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into transformer layers while keeping pre-trained weights frozen. Simple, effective, widely adopted.

QLoRA (Quantized LoRA)

Combines 4-bit quantization with LoRA, enabling fine-tuning of 65B parameter models on a single GPU.

AdaLoRA

Adaptively allocates parameter budgets based on importance scoring, achieving better performance with fewer parameters.

IA³ (Infused Adapter by Inhibiting and Amplifying Inner Activations)

Scales inner activations of pretrained models with three tiny learnable vectors per layer.

Code Example

from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model

config = LoraConfig(
 r=16,
 lora_alpha=32,
 target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],
 lora_dropout=0.1,
)
model = get_peft_model(base_model, config)
model.print_trainable_parameters()
# trainable params: 4,194,304 || all params: 7,424,243,712

Benchmark Results

On the GLUE benchmark, LoRA achieves 98.2% of full fine-tuning performance with 0.1% of trainable parameters. QLoRA maintains this with 4-bit quantization, reducing memory usage by 75%.

item.title: OpenAI Assistant API — Open-Source Implementation
item.description: A production-grade open-source implementation of the OpenAI Assistant API with full compatibility for threads, messages, tools, and vector stores. Features custom model routing, local deployment support, and enhanced streaming capabilities.

item.title: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models via Adaptive Low-Rank Adaptation
Building Production-Ready RAG Systems
item.description:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become the standard architecture for enterprise AI applications. Here are key lessons from our production deployments.

Chunking Strategy Matters More Than You Think

The way you split documents into chunks dramatically affects retrieval quality. We found that semantic chunking (splitting at natural boundaries identified by embeddings) outperforms fixed-size chunking by 23% on our benchmarks.

Hybrid Search is Essential

Pure dense retrieval misses exact keyword matches. Pure sparse retrieval misses semantic similarity. Combining both with learned weighting gives the best results across diverse query types.

Evaluation Framework

Don’t evaluate RAG with generic benchmarks. Build a domain-specific evaluation set with:

  • Representative queries from your actual users
  • Ground-truth relevant documents for each query
  • Clear success criteria (both precision and recall matter)

Monitoring in Production

Track these metrics continuously:

  • Retrieval precision@k
  • Generation quality scores
  • Latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99)
  • Error rates by query type

item.title: Building Production-Ready RAG Systems
item.description:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become the standard architecture for enterprise AI applications. Here are key lessons from our production deployments.

Chunking Strategy Matters More Than You Think

The way you split documents into chunks dramatically affects retrieval quality. We found that semantic chunking (splitting at natural boundaries identified by embeddings) outperforms fixed-size chunking by 23% on our benchmarks.

Hybrid Search is Essential

Pure dense retrieval misses exact keyword matches. Pure sparse retrieval misses semantic similarity. Combining both with learned weighting gives the best results across diverse query types.

Evaluation Framework

Don’t evaluate RAG with generic benchmarks. Build a domain-specific evaluation set with:

  • Representative queries from your actual users
  • Ground-truth relevant documents for each query
  • Clear success criteria (both precision and recall matter)

Monitoring in Production

Track these metrics continuously:

  • Retrieval precision@k
  • Generation quality scores
  • Latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99)
  • Error rates by query type

item.title: NeuralChat — Lightweight Conversational AI Framework
item.description: A lightweight framework for building and deploying conversational AI systems. Supports multiple backend providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models) with a unified API interface, custom fine-tuning pipelines, and RAG integration.

item.title: Multimodal Reasoning in Vision-Language Models: Benchmarks and Baselines
AutoML Pipeline Toolkit
item.description: An automated machine learning toolkit featuring Bayesian optimization, evolutionary algorithms, and neural architecture search. Built on PyTorch with GPU acceleration and distributed training support.

item.title: Self-Supervised Representation Learning for Scientific Document Understanding
Principal Investigator & Lead Researcher
item.description: Founded and lead the AI Research Laboratory, managing a team of 8 researchers across NLP, computer vision, and reinforcement learning. Published 12+ peer-reviewed papers at top venues (ACL, NeurIPS, ICML). Secured $500K+ in research funding.

item.title: Senior Research Scientist
item.description: Led a team of 5 researchers on LLM fine-tuning, RAG systems, and multimodal AI. Developed novel parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods adopted by 100+ teams. Published 8 papers at top-tier conferences.

item.title: Research Intern
item.description: Worked on multi-agent reinforcement learning for resource allocation problems. Co-authored paper accepted at ICML 2021. Developed novel reward shaping techniques that improved convergence speed by 40%.
